title: SPARC Retrograde Neuroanatomical Tracing of Phrenic Motor Neurons Using Intrapleural Injections of Cholera Toxin B Fragment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2rbg6","text":"This protocol describes the procedure for retrogradely labeling bilateral phrenic motor neuron pools in the cervical spinal cord using intrapleural injections of cholera toxin B fragment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Anesthesia is induced in a closed chamber (3.5%isoflurane in 100%O2) then transfered to a heated surgical station and anesthesia is maintained via nose cone at 2-2.5%
Sterilized 25 μL Hamilton syringes with 9.52-mm sterile needles are prepared to administer CtB intrapleurally in rats.
Cholera toxin B fragment (CtB) is prepared as follows: 0.2% weight/volume; dissolved in sterile H2O; Calbiochem, Billerica, MA.
25 μL of CtB is loaded into a 25-μL Hamilton syringe and 12.5 μL is injected per side (25μL total per animal).
Prep injection site for injection using aseptic methods:
shave and scrub injection sites per Institutionally approved animal protocol
Rats were turned sideways to inject CtB at the 5th intercostal space at a depth of ~6 mm.
Following injections, discontinue isoflurane and allow rat to recover fully while monitoring for signs of respiratory compromise or distress
creator: Elisa Gonzalez-Rothi
